items: Grants list now refreshes automatically every 20 seconds, so changes made by coworkers (claims, status moves, newly scraped grants) show up without a manual page reload. Pauses while a grant card is open or the tab is in the background.
Sped up the dashboard's status/claim counts by combining several separate database queries into two, and added database indexes on grant status fields — most noticeable on larger grant lists.

Fixed a bug where deleting your account would fail with a generic error page if you had ever claimed a grant or taken any other tracked action. Account deletion now works correctly in that case, and shows a clear message instead of a broken page if it ever can't complete.
Grant history now shows the name of a team member who has since deleted their account (e.g. "Jane Doe (deleted)") instead of attributing their past actions to "System".

Team members can now claim a grant to signal they're working on it, release it, or take over a grant claimed by someone else. Claimed grants show a badge on their card, and claim status (mine / available / claimed) is now filterable from the dashboard.
Added a Reviewed stage to the grant status ladder (New → Reviewed → Applied), plus a matching filter, count, and actions in the grant detail view.

Added an "All" option and a new filter sidebar with live counts for status, claim, source, deadline window, and minimum match score — replacing the old filter bar. The sidebar can be collapsed for more room and works as a slide-out drawer on mobile.
Renamed the "Relevant" status label to "New" throughout the dashboard and grant detail view for clarity.
Grants you've already claimed, reviewed, applied to, starred, or added notes to are no longer overwritten by the next scrape — only the scrape timestamp updates, so your work on a grant sticks.
Improved filtering of irrelevant scraped results (e.g. unrelated fishery grants) so fewer off-topic grants show up in the list.

First production release of the grants dashboard on its new Laravel-based platform, migrated from the previous system. Includes user accounts, role-based access, team management, and forced password change on first login.
